tool.name: dreg
tool.summary: scan nucleotide sequences with deterministic bounded regular expressions

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `dreg` searches nucleotide sequence records with a bounded regular expression. EMBOSS-RS v1 applies a Rust regular expression case-insensitively to normalized nucleotide residues and reports overlapping forward-strand matches only.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local nucleotide input and one regular-expression pattern. Protein-classified inputs are rejected. Unknown-classified residues are treated literally in the subject sequence; there is no ambiguity-expansion layer.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a deterministic table with record identifier, pattern text, 1-based inclusive start and end coordinates, and the matched normalized residue slice.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs dreg`. Validation currently covers overlapping regex hits against a committed nucleotide FASTA fixture through the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not search reverse complements, does not expose EMBOSS-era fuzzy mismatch semantics, and rejects empty or zero-width regular expressions.

artifacts.label: Dreg nucleotide FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed nucleotide FASTA fixture containing one record with overlapping `ATA` matches.

examples.title: Report overlapping forward-strand regex hits
examples.description: Runs `dreg` against a committed nucleotide FASTA fixture and reports overlapping `ATA` matches from the forward sequence.

expected_outputs.label: Overlapping nucleotide regex hit table
expected_outputs.description: Two overlapping hits are reported from record `dregA` with 1-based inclusive coordinates.
